records.chapter: Reward of near ones who cry out of fear of Allah, worship Him keeping away from what He has prohibited and having beauty of abstinence in this world
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub who said it was narrated to me by Abu Ayoob’ from al-Wasafi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “O Musa! There is no one to receive nearness like those among the near ones who cry out of My fear. There is no worshipper like one who worships Me keeping away from what I have prohibited. There is no one who beautifies Me more than those who abstain because they do not beautify themselves with those things they are independent of.” Musa (a.s.) asked the Lord, “O the Most Merciful of the merciful ones! What is the reward of those people?” Allah replied, “O Musa! Those who seek nearness fearing Me will be with My best friends where no ordinary person can come. I feel ashamed to inquire about the deeds of those who worship Me keeping away from what I have prohibited. One who seeks nearness to Me through abstinence in this world I will grant him every kind of paradise so that he may live wherever he likes.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو أَیُّوبَ عَنِ الْوَصَّافِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: فِیمَا نَاجَی بِهِ اللَّهُ مُوسَی ع عَلَی الطُّورِ أَنْ یَا مُوسَی أَبْلِغْ قَوْمَکَ أَنَّهُ مَا یَتَقَرَّبُ إِلَیَّ الْمُتَقَرِّبُونَ بِمِثْلِ الْبُکَاءِ مِنْ خَشْیَتِی وَ مَا تَعَبَّدَ إِلَیَّ الْمُتَعَبِّدُونَ بِمِثْلِ الْوَرَعِ عَنْ مَحَارِمِی وَ لَا تَزَیَّنَ لِیَ الْمُتَزَیِّنُونَ بِمِثْلِ الزُّهْدِ فِی الدُّنْیَا عَمَّا بِهِمُ الْغِنَی عَنْهُ قَالَ فَقَالَ مُوسَی ع یَا أَکْرَمَ الْأَکْرَمِینَ فَمَا ذَا أَثَبْتَهُمْ عَلَی ذَلِکَ فَقَالَ یَا مُوسَی أَمَّا الْمُتَقَرِّبُونَ إِلَیَّ بِالْبُکَاءِ مِنْ خَشْیَتِی فَهُمْ فِی الرَّفِیقِ الْأَعْلَی لَا یُشَارِکُهُمْ فِیهِ أَحَدٌ وَ أَمَّا الْمُتَعَبِّدُونَ إِلَیَّ بِالْوَرَعِ مِنْ مَحَارِمِی فإن [فَإِنِّی] أُفَتِّشُ النَّاسَ عَلَی أَعْمَالِهِمْ وَ لَا أُفَتِّشُهُمْ حَیَاءً مِنْهُمْ وَ أَمَّا الْمُتَقَرِّبُونَ إِلَیَّ بِالزُّهْدِ فِی الدُّنْیَا فَإِنِّی أَمْنَحُهُمُ الْجَنَّهَ بِحَذَافِیرِهَا یتبوءوا [یَتَبَوَّءُونَ] مِنْهَا حَیْثُ شاء [یَشَاءُونَ].
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub who said it was narrated to me by Abu Ayoob’ from al-Wasafi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “O Musa! There is no one to receive nearness like those among the near ones who cry out of My fear. There is no worshipper like one who worships Me keeping away from what I have prohibited. There is no one who beautifies Me more than those who abstain because they do not beautify themselves with those things they are independent of.” Musa (a.s.) asked the Lord, “O the Most Merciful of the merciful ones! What is the reward of those people?” Allah replied, “O Musa! Those who seek nearness fearing Me will be with My best friends where no ordinary person can come. I feel ashamed to inquire about the deeds of those who worship Me keeping away from what I have prohibited. One who seeks nearness to Me through abstinence in this world I will grant him every kind of paradise so that he may live wherever he likes.”

records.chapter: Reward of doing good to a believer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Abi Walad from Maysar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “On the day of Judgment, a believer will pass by a person whom he had known in the world. An angel will be appointed to take that person to Hell and he will call out to the believer, ‘Please help me for I had done good to you and fulfilled your desire in the world. Can you repay that good deed of mine today?’ The believer will ask the angels to leave that person. Upon hearing the desire of that believer, Allah will order the angels to obey the believer and leave that person alone.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِی وَلَّادٍ عَنْ مُیَسِّرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ مِنْکُمْ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ لَیَمُرُّ بِهِ الرَّجُلُ لَهُ الْمَعْرِفَهُ بِهِ فِی الدُّنْیَا وَ قَدْ أُمِرَ بِهِ إِلَی النَّارِ وَ الْمَلَکُ یَنْطَلِقُ بِهِ قَالَ فَیَقُولُ یَا فُلَانُ أَغِثْنِی فَقَدْ کُنْتُ أَصْنَعُ إِلَیْکَ الْمَعْرُوفَ فِی الدُّنْیَا وَ أُسْعِفُکَ فِی الْحَاجَهِ تَطْلُبُهَا مِنِّی فَهَلْ مِنْ عِنْدِکَ الْیَوْمَ مُکَافَاهٌ فَیَقُولُ الْمُؤْمِنُ لِلْمَلَکِ الْمُوَکَّلِ بِهِ خَلِّ سَبِیلَهُ قَالَ فلیسمع [فَیَسْمَعُ] اللَّهُ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِ فَیَأْمُرُ الْمَلَکَ أَنْ یُجِیزَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِ فَیُخَلِّیَ سَبِیلَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Abi Walad from Maysar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “On the day of Judgment, a believer will pass by a person whom he had known in the world. An angel will be appointed to take that person to Hell and he will call out to the believer, ‘Please help me for I had done good to you and fulfilled your desire in the world. Can you repay that good deed of mine today?’ The believer will ask the angels to leave that person. Upon hearing the desire of that believer, Allah will order the angels to obey the believer and leave that person alone.”

records.chapter: Reward of expecting good from Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Abdulrahman b. al-Hajjaj from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “If a servant has good expectations from Allah he will be rewarded according to his expectations. And one who expects bad will be meted out according to his ill expectations. This is the meaning of the words of Allah, the Mighty and Sublime: And that was your (evil) thought which you entertained about your Lord that has tumbled you down into perdition, so are you become of the lost ones.
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ آخِرَ عَبْدٍ یُؤْمَرُ بِهِ إِلَی النَّارِ فَیَلْتَفِتُ فَیَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَعْجِلُوهُ فَإِذَا أُتِیَ بِهِ قَالَ لَهُ عَبْدِی لِمَ الْتَفَتَّ فَیَقُولُ یَا رَبِّ مَا کَانَ ظَنِّی بِکَ هَذَا فَیَقُولُ اللَّهُ جَلَّ جَلَالُهُ عَبْدِی وَ مَا کَانَ ظَنُّکَ بِی فَیَقُولُ یَا رَبِّ کَانَ ظَنِّی بِکَ أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِی خَطِیئَتِی وَ تُدْخِلَنِی جَنَّتَکَ فَیَقُولُ اللَّهُ مَلَائِکَتِی وَ عِزَّتِی وَ جَلَالِی وَ بَلَائِی وَ ارْتِفَاعِ مَکَانِی مَا ظَنَّ بِی هَذَا سَاعَهً مِنْ حَیَاتِهِ خَیْراً قَطُّ وَ لَوْ ظَنَّ بِی سَاعَهً مِنْ حَیَاتِهِ خَیْراً مَا رَوَّعْتُهُ بِالنَّارِ أَجِیزُوا لَهُ کَذِبَهُ وَ أَدْخِلُوهُ الْجَنَّهَ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَا ظَنَّ عَبْدٌ بِاللَّهِ خَیْراً إِلَّا کَانَ عِنْدَ ظَنِّهِ بِهِ وَ ذَلِکَ قَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ وَ ذلِکُمْ ظَنُّکُمُ الَّذِی ظَنَنْتُمْ بِرَبِّکُمْ أَرْداکُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُمْ مِنَ الْخاسِرِینَ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Abdulrahman b. al-Hajjaj from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If a servant has good expectations from Allah he will be rewarded according to his expectations. And one who expects bad will be meted out according to his ill expectations. This is the meaning of the words of Allah, the Mighty and Sublime: And that was your (evil) thought which you entertained about your Lord that has tumbled you down into perdition, so are you become of the lost ones.

records.chapter: Reward of cultivating the advices of Allah in one’s self
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muawiyah from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “No Muslim man cultivates the advices of Allah in himself then gives the rights and recovers the rights; but that he gets two benefits – he will get satisfying sustenance and salvation-giving pleasure of Allah.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا نَاصَحَ اللَّهَ عَبْدٌ مُسْلِمٌ فِی نَفْسِهِ فَأَعْطَی الْحَقَّ مِنْهَا وَ أَخَذَ الْحَقَّ لَهَا [إِلَّا] أُعْطِیَ خَصْلَتَیْنِ رِزْقاً مِنَ اللَّهِ یَقْنَعُ بِهِ وَ یَرْضَی مِنَ اللَّهِ بِتَحِیَّتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Muawiyah from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “No Muslim man cultivates the advices of Allah in himself then gives the rights and recovers the rights; but that he gets two benefits – he will get satisfying sustenance and salvation-giving pleasure of Allah.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ridha  (a.s.)  narrates  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.) said:  “One  who  wears  a  cornelian  ring  on  his  finger  will never be needy and his desires will be fulfilled in the best way.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنِ اتَّخَذَ خَاتَماً فَصُّهُ عَقِیقٌ لَمْ یَفْتَقِرْ وَ لَمْ یُقْضَ لَهُ إِلَّا بِالَّتِی هِیَ أَحْسَنُ

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Ibrahim b. ’Uqba from Sayabeh b. Ayoob’ from Muhammad b. al-Fadhl from Abd al-Raheem al-Qaseer who said: “Give a cornelian ring to him.” The order of Imam was obeyed and a ring was given to him. And because of this, he didn’t have to face any harm from that unjust ruler.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ سَیَابَهَ بْنِ أَیُّوبَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفَضْلِ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحِیمِ الْقَصِیرِ قَالَ: بَعَثَ الْوَالِی إِلَی رَجُلٍ مِنْ آلِ أَبِی طَالِبٍ فِی حیاته [جِنَایَهٍ] فَمَرَّ بِأَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فَقَالَ أَتْبِعُوهُ بِخَاتَمِ عَقِیقٍ قَالَ فَأُتْبِعَ بِخَاتَمِ عَقِیقٍ فَلَمْ یَرَ مَکْرُوهاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Ibrahim b. ’Uqba from Sayabeh b. Ayoob’ from Muhammad b. al-Fadhl from Abd al-Raheem al-Qaseer who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Give a cornelian ring to him.” The order of Imam was obeyed and a ring was given to him. And because of this, he didn’t have to face any harm from that unjust ruler.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Abi Junub from his father from Umar b. [Abi] al-Miqdam from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Where is his cornelian ring? If he had been wearing that, he would not have been lashed.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِی جَنُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ [أَبِی] الْمِقْدَامِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَرَّ بِهِ رَجُلٌ مَجْلُودٌ فَقَالَ أَیْنَ کَانَ خَاتَمُهُ الْعَقِیقُ أَمَا إِنَّهُ لَوْ کَانَ عَلَیْهِ مَا جُلِدَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Abi Junub from his father from Umar b. [Abi] al-Miqdam from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Where is his cornelian ring? If he had been wearing that, he would not have been lashed.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 4.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “Cornelian is an amulet in journey.”
records.arabicText: وَ رُوِیَ فِی حَدِیثٍ آخَرَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع الْعَقِیقُ حِرْزٌ فِی السَّفَرِ.

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 5.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said, “Keep a cornelian ring in your hand  so  that  Allah  gives  you  abundance  and  you  remain safe from calamities.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ مَزْیَدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ: تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْعَقِیقِ یُبَارِکِ اللَّهُ عَلَیْکُمْ وَ تَکُونُوا فِی أَمْنٍ مِنَ الْبَلَاءِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Keep a cornelian ring in your hand  so  that  Allah  gives  you  abundance  and  you  remain safe from calamities.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 6. And from the previous chain who said: “Why didn’t you wear a cornelian ring? It protects one from all evils?”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ: شَکَا رَجُلٌ إِلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص أَنَّهُ قُطِعَ عَلَیْهِ الطَّرِیقُ فَقَالَ لَهُ هَلَّا تَخَتَّمْتَ بِالْعَقِیقِ فَإِنَّهُ یُحْرَزُ مِنْ کُلِّ سُوءٍ وَ فِی حَدِیثٍ آخَرَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع مَنْ تَخَتَّمَ بِالْعَقِیقِ لَمْ یَزَلْ یَنْظُرُ الْحُسْنَی مَا دَامَ فِی یَدِهِ وَ لَمْ یَزَلْ عَلَیْهِ مِنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَاقِیَهٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 6. And from the previous chain who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Why didn’t you wear a cornelian ring? It protects one from all evils?”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 7. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah who said it was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Musa al-Khashab from ’Aqeel b. al-Mutawakil al-Makki who raised it to from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his grandfather (a.s.) who said: Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that: “One who wears a ring inscribed ‘Muh’ammadun nabiyyullaahi wa a’liyyunw waliyyullaah’ Allah will save him from unpleasant death and he will die a natural death only.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَی الْخَشَّابُ عَنْ عَقِیلِ بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ الْمَکِّیِّ یَرْفَعُهُ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ صَاغَ خَاتَماً عَقِیقاً فَنَقَشَ فِیهِ مُحَمَّدٌ نَبِیُّ اللَّهِ وَ عَلِیٌّ وَلِیُّ اللَّهِ وَقَاهُ اللَّهُ مِیتَهَ السَّوْءِ وَ لَمْ یَمُتْ إِلَّا عَلَی الْفِطْرَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 7. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah who said it was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Musa al-Khashab from ’Aqeel b. al-Mutawakil al-Makki who raised it to from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his grandfather (a.s.) who said: Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who wears a ring inscribed ‘Muh’ammadun nabiyyullaahi wa a’liyyunw waliyyullaah’ Allah will save him from unpleasant death and he will die a natural death only.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 8. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ali b. al-Rayan from Ali b. Muhammad b. Ishaq al-Shaybani who raised it to Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “No hand raised in front of Allah is loved by Him more than one which has a cornelian in it.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الرَّیَّانِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ الشَّیْبَانِیِّ رَفَعَهُ إِلَی أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا رُفِعَتْ کَفٌّ إِلَی اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ- أَحَبُّ إِلَیْهِ مِنْ کَفٍّ فِیهَا عَقِیقٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 8. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ali b. al-Rayan from Ali b. Muhammad b. Ishaq al-Shaybani who raised it to Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “No hand raised in front of Allah is loved by Him more than one which has a cornelian in it.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 9. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hussain b. Ali b. bint Ilyas al-Khazaz from al-Ridha (a.s.) who said: “One who seeks sustenance through cornelian will get a huge share.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ بِنْتِ إِلْیَاسَ الْخَزَّازِ عَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ: مَنْ سَاهَمَ بِالْعَقِیقِ کَانَ سَهْمُهُ الْأَوْفَرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 9. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hussain b. Ali b. bint Ilyas al-Khazaz from al-Ridha (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who seeks sustenance through cornelian will get a huge share.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing cornelian (Aqiq) ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 10. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Ali al-Aqooli from Ahmad b. Harun al-’Attar from Ziyad al-’Abdi from Musa b. Ja’far from his father Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father Muhammad b. Ali from his father Ali b. al-Hussain from his father al-Hussain b. Ali (a.s.) who said: ‘I swear by Myself, I have made it obligatory on Myself that I will never punish with fire (of Hell) one who wears cornelian in his hand and is the lover of Ali (a.s.).”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ الْعَاقُولِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هَارُونَ الْعَطَّارِ عَنْ زِیَادٍ الْعَبْدِیِّ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ ع قَالَ: لَمَّا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مُوسَی بْنَ عِمْرَانَ کَلَّمَهُ عَلَی طُورِ سِینَا ثُمَّ اطَّلَعَ عَلَی الْأَرْضِ اطِّلَاعَهً فَخَلَقَ مِنْ نُورِ وَجْهِهِ الْعَقِیقَ ثُمَّ قَالَ آلَیْتُ بِنَفْسِی عَلَی نَفْسِی أَلَّا أُعَذِّبَ کَفَّ لَابِسِهِ إِذَا تَوَلَّی عَلِیّاً بِالنَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 10. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by al-Hassan b. Ali al-Aqooli from Ahmad b. Harun al-’Attar from Ziyad al-’Abdi from Musa b. Ja’far from his father Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father Muhammad b. Ali from his father Ali b. al-Hussain from his father al-Hussain b. Ali (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: ‘I swear by Myself, I have made it obligatory on Myself that I will never punish with fire (of Hell) one who wears cornelian in his hand and is the lover of Ali (a.s.).”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing a turquoise ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “The hand having a turquoise ring will never be needy.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمُؤْمِنِ الْأَنْصَارِیِّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ مَا افْتَقَرَتْ کَفٌّ تَخَتَّمَتْ بِالْفَیْرُوزَجِ.

records.chapter: Reward of wearing a turquoise ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from Muhammad b. Ahmad who said it was narrated to me by Abu Ya’qub Yusif b. al-Sakht from al-Hassan b. Sahl al-Basri from Ali b. Mahziyar who said: “It is Zafar.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو یَعْقُوبَ یُوسُفُ بْنُ السُّخْتِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ سَهْلٍ الْبَصْرِیِّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَهْزِیَارَ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَی أَبِی الْحَسَنِ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ ع فَرَأَیْتُ فِی یَدِهِ خَاتَماً فَصُّهُ فَیْرُوزَجٌ نَقْشُهُ لِلَّهِ الْمُلْکُ فَأَدَمْتُ النَّظَرَ إِلَیْهِ فَقَالَ مَا لَکَ تَنْظُرُ فِیهِ هَذَا حَجَرٌ أَهْدَاهُ جَبْرَئِیلُ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص مِنَ الْجَنَّهِ فَوَهَبَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لِعَلِیٍّ ع أَ تَدْرِی مَا اسْمُهُ قَالَ قُلْتُ فَیْرُوزَجٌ قَالَ هَذَا اسْمُهُ بِالْفَارِسِیَّهِ أَ تَعْرِفُ اسْمَهُ بِالْعَرَبِیَّهِ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَا قَالَ هُوَ الظَّفَرُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from Muhammad b. Ahmad who said it was narrated to me by Abu Ya’qub Yusif b. al-Sakht from al-Hassan b. Sahl al-Basri from Ali b. Mahziyar who said:

records.chapter: Reward of wearing Jiz-e-Yamani
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Amirul  Momineen  (a.s.)  said,  “Wear  a  ring  of  Jiz-e- Yamani  in  your  hand  for  the  deceit  of  Satan  is destroyed through it.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عُبَیْدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْجَزْعِ الْیَمَانِیِّ فَإِنَّهُ یَرُدُّ کَیْدَ مَرَدَهِ الشَّیَاطِینِ.

records.chapter: Reward of wearing an Emerald ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated from Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) who read out from  a  book:  “There  are  lots  of  comforts  for  a  person wearing the Emerald ring and there is no hardship for him.” The narrator wrote down these words of Imam (a.s.).
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی الْحُسَیْنُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا یُلَقَّبُ بِسِکْبَاجَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ نَصْرٍ صَاحِبِ الْأَتْرَاکِ وَ کَانَ یَقُومُ بِبَعْضِ أُمُورِ الْمَاضِی ع قَالَ: قَالَ یَوْماً وَ أَمْلَاهُ عَلَیَّ مِنْ کِتَابِ التَّخَتُّمِ التَّخَتُّمُ بِالزُّمُرُّدِ یُسْرٌ لَا عُسْرَ فِیهِ.

records.chapter: Reward of wearing a Ruby ring
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ridha  (a.s.)  narrates  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.) said, “Wear a Ruby ring for it puts an end to poverty.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبِی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ کَانَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ تَخَتَّمُوا بِالْیَوَاقِیتِ فَإِنَّهُ یَنْفِی الْفَقْرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam  Ridha  (a.s.)  narrates  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Wear a Ruby ring for it puts an end to poverty.”

records.chapter: Reward of wearing a ring of Billaur (Crystal)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “Billaur is an excellent gem.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الرَّیَّانِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمَعْرُوفِ بِابْنِ وَهْبٍ الْعَبْدِیِّ قَرْیَهٌ مِنْ قُرَی وَاسِطٍ یَرْفَعُهُ إِلَی أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: نِعْمَ الْفَصُّ الْبِلَّوْرُ.

records.chapter: Reward of humility
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Sadaqah from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father (a.s.) that Ali (a.s.) who said: Keep your head up. There is a lot of honor for you near Allah and you won’t be disgraced due to your humility.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ ع أَنَّ عَلِیّاً ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ وُلْدِ آدَمَ إِلَّا وَ نَاصِیَتُهُ بِیَدِ مَلَکٍ فَإِنْ تَکَبَّرَ جَذَبَهُ بِنَاصِیَتِهِ إِلَی الْأَرْضِ وَ قَالَ لَهُ تَوَاضَعْ وَضَعَکَ اللَّهُ وَ إِنْ تَوَاضَعَ جَذَبَهُ بِنَاصِیَتِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ ارْفَعْ رَأْسَکَ رَفَعَکَ اللَّهُ وَ لَا وَضَعَکَ بِتَوَاضُعِکَ لِلَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Sadaqah from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father (a.s.) that Ali (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Keep your head up. There is a lot of honor for you near Allah and you won’t be disgraced due to your humility.”

records.chapter: Reward of crying out of fear of Allah, keeping away from what He has prohibited and remaining awake at night in the way of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “All the eyes will cry on the Day of Judgment except three – an eye which cried out of fear of Allah, an eye which remained awake for the sake of Allah and an eye which refrained from seeing what Allah has prohibited.” The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “Tooba (Congratulations) be for the face to look at which is so nice whose eyes are weeping over sins committed by him that are not known to anyone.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغِیرَهِ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص کُلُّ عَیْنٍ بَاکِیَهٌ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ إِلَّا ثَلَاثَهَ أَعْیُنٍ عَیْنٌ بَکَتْ مِنْ خَشْیَهِ اللَّهِ وَ عَیْنٌ بَاتَتْ سَاهِرَهً فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ وَ عَیْنٌ غُضَّتْ عَنْ مَحَارِمِ اللَّهِ وَ قَالَ ص طُوبَی لِصُورَهٍ نَظَرَ اللَّهُ إِلَیْهَا تَبْکِی عَلَی ذَنْبٍ مِنْ خَشْیَهِ اللَّهِ لَمْ یَطَّلِعْ عَلَی ذَلِکَ الذَّنْبِ غَیْرُهُ.

records.chapter: Reward of avoiding lust today for the sake of those promised things which are unseen
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “Tooba (Congratulations) be for one who avoids lust today for the sake of those promised things he has not seen.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الْکُوفِیُّ عَنْ جَدِّهِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغِیرَهِ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص طُوبَی لِمَنْ تَرَکَ شَهْوَهً حَاضِرَهً لِمَوْعُودٍ لَمْ یَرَهُ.

records.chapter: Reward of loving, building mosques and repenting at dawn for the sake of Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Kufi from his father from Abdillah b. al-Mughira from al-Sakuni from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers (a.s.) who said: “But for those who love each other, build mosques and repent at dawn, I would surely have chastised the people of the earth.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ الْکُوفِیُّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغِیرَهِ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ یُصِیبَ أَهْلَ الْأَرْضِ بِعَذَابٍ یَقُولُ لَوْ لَا الَّذِینَ یَتَحَابُّونَ فِیَّ وَ یَعْمُرُونَ مَسَاجِدِی وَ یَسْتَغْفِرُونَ بِالْأَسْحَارِ لَوْلَاهُمْ لَأَنْزَلْتُ عَلَیْهِمْ عَذَابِی.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Kufi from his father from Abdillah b. al-Mughira from al-Sakuni from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “But for those who love each other, build mosques and repent at dawn, I would surely have chastised the people of the earth.”

records.chapter: Reward of that person whose seeing is a lesson, silence is thought, conversation is remembrance of Allah, who weeps over his sins and from whom people remain safe
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Muhammad  Baqir  (a.s.)  narrates  that  Amirul Momineen  (a.s.)  said,  “All  the  good  deeds  are  hidden  in three  qualities  –  seeing,  silence  and  speech.  One  who’s seeing is not a lesson is useless. One who is not a fountain of silence and deep thought is ignorant. One whose speech is  not  the  remembrance  of  Allah  is  worthless.  A  person whose seeing is a lesson, silence is thought, conversation is remembrance  of  Allah,  who  cries  over  his  sins  and  from whom  people  remain  safe,  is  indeed  fortunate  (there  is Tooba for him).”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ یُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ أَبِی أَیُّوبَ الْحَرَّانِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع جُمِعَ الْخَیْرُ کُلُّهُ فِی ثَلَاثِ خِصَالٍ النَّظَرِ وَ السُّکُوتِ وَ الْکَلَامِ- وَ کُلُّ نَظَرٍ لَیْسَ فِیهِ اعْتِبَارٌ فَهُوَ سَهْوٌ وَ کُلُّ سُکُوتٍ لَیْسَ فِیهِ فِکْرٌ فَهُوَ غَفْلَهٌ وَ کُلُّ کَلَامٍ لَیْسَ فِیهِ ذِکْرٌ فَهُوَ لَغْوٌ طُوبَی لِمَنْ کَانَ نَظَرُهُ عِبْرَهً وَ سُکُوتُهُ فکر [فِکْرَهً] وَ کَلَامُهُ ذکر [ذِکْراً] وَ بَکَی عَلَی خَطِیئَتِهِ وَ أَمِنَ النَّاسُ شَرَّهُ.
